title: Universal Right-Hand Chirality of Evanescent Vector Fields
authors: Yilin Qing, Ji Zou, Yi-Pu Wang, Gerrit E. W. Bauer, Tao Yu
abstract: A locking between propagation direction $\hat{\bf q}$, spin $\hat{\bf S}$, and surface or interface normal $\hat{\bf n}$ -- a phenomenon broadly termed \textit{chirality} -- pervades evanescent or surface waves in optics, magnetism, plasmonics, and acoustics. Yet, it is not known whether the phenomenon is universal or bound to conditions. Here, we unveil that any \textit{source-free} vector field that propagates along $\hat{\bf q}$ and is evanescent along $\hat{\bf n}$, the existence of a spin automatically enforces a rigid, right-handed locking among $\hat{\bf q}$, $\hat{\bf S}$, and $\hat{\bf n}$, that is characterized by a chirality index $C_{\bf q}\equiv \hat{\bf n}\cdot(\hat{\bf S}\times \hat{\bf q})>0$. For an arbitrary propagation direction defined by $\hat{\bf q}\cdot \hat{\bf S}\equiv η\in [-1,1]$, i.e., not necessarily transverse spin, we derive a fundamental upper bound $C_{\bf q}\le \sqrt{1-η^2}$. The universal absence of left-handed evanescent (source-less) vector fields underscores the fundamental physical non-equivalence of mirror-image configurations -- a principle echoed by parity violation, the natural excess of enantiomers in chiral molecules, and the fixed chirality of DNA.
summary: This work establishes a universal physical law stating that any source-free, evanescent vector field must possess a fixed, right-handed chirality defined by the relationship between its propagation direction, spin, and surface normal. The authors derive a fundamental upper bound for this chirality, demonstrating that the absence of left-handed configurations underscores a deep physical non-equivalence between mirror-image wave states.
main_problem: To determine the general conditions under which an evanescent vector field possesses definite chirality and to prove a universal upper bound for this chirality.
main_result: The paper proves that any source-free, evanescent vector field must exhibit a rigid, right-handed chirality ($C_q > 0$), bounded by $C_q \le \sqrt{1 - \eta^2}$.
method:
The derivation uses the source-free condition ($
abla \cdot \mathbf{V} = 0$) applied to time-harmonic evanescent plane wave modes to constrain the relationship between propagation ($\hat{\mathbf{q}}$), spin ($\hat{\mathbf{S}}$), and surface normal ($\hat{\mathbf{n}}$).
model_or_system: The analysis focuses on source-free, time-harmonic evanescent vector fields, applicable to various physical systems like surface plasmons, spin waves, and acoustic waves at interfaces.
key_observables: The chirality index, $C_q \equiv \hat{\mathbf{n}} \cdot (\hat{\mathbf{S}} 	imes \hat{\mathbf{q}})$, and the spin-orbit parameter, $\eta = \hat{\mathbf{q}} \cdot \hat{\mathbf{S}} / |\mathbf{q}||\mathbf{S}|$.
important_parameters: The chirality index $C_q$ is bounded by $\sqrt{1 - \eta^2}$; the analysis is simplified under the approximation $ eta \ll |\mathbf{q}|$.
main_assumptions:
The field must be source-free ($
abla \cdot \mathbf{V} = 0$) and time-harmonic ($e^{-i\Omega t}$).
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates the universal right-handed rule and the general bound $0 \le C_q \le \sqrt{1 - \eta^2}$. Table I summarizes the chirality index for various prototypical evanescent waves, confirming the universal chirality.
paper_structure: The paper establishes the physical problem of chirality in evanescent waves, derives the mathematical constraints using the source-free condition, proves the universal right-handed nature and the upper bound, and discusses the physical implications of this chirality.
why_interesting: The concept of universal chirality locking in fundamental wave phenomena (like surface waves) provides a powerful, model-independent diagnostic tool for distinguishing between source-free physical regimes and those containing internal sources or higher-order multipoles.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
